statement: When a repository keeps durable language-specific guidance for coding agents, that guidance should be anchored in authoritative references and carry a visible freshness date. Use community anecdotes for pattern recognition only; do not let unsourced forum or blog advice become the primary basis for persistent instruction files.
publicSummary: Persistent language guidance should prefer authoritative references plus a last-verified date over unsourced community lore.
cautionNotes: Generalized from durable C++ guidance curation. Applies to other language-specific instruction sets when official references exist.
nonGeneralizationNotes: Does not prescribe any specific language rules; it only constrains how persistent guidance should be sourced.
